top_signals.title: Why Ebola came back — and a warning for the next pandemic
top_signals.summary: The outbreak in the DR Congo is unlikely to spread worldwide. But it comes at a time of cuts to health budgets and exposes a worrying lack of preparedness for ‘Disease X’
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Goldman and JPMorgan ease office working rules to counter World Cup disruption
top_signals.summary: Wall Street lenders say employees can ask to work from home on match days to ease congestion in host cities
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: How video games became a channel for political influence
top_signals.summary: From China to Saudi Arabia, governments are investing billions in gaming to project influence and assert control
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The consequences of online race hatred are playing out on Belfast’s streets
top_signals.summary: Malign social media automates division — I saw the effects in our city this week

top_signals.title: European tech sovereignty: a doubtful goal but a good investment
top_signals.summary: Given the continent’s weak starting point, its modest ambitions could have a big impact on local companies

top_signals.title: What Andy Burnham could mean for your money
top_signals.summary: The Labour leadership hopeful has left clues as to his priorities on tax, investment and housing

top_signals.title: France opposes ‘anglicisation’ of EU trade talks
top_signals.summary: Trade commissioner argues translation into 24 languages slows down bloc’s ability to strike up deals
